page: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU architecture includes an L1d cache of 32 KB, L2 cache of 1.0 MB, and L3 cache of 32 MB, with support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16.

This server configuration does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments. It also does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The virtualization layer is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

In benchmark evaluations, the D48as_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core and single-core performance. Single-core stress-ng CPU performance ranks in the top tier, while multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall within the top 25%. Memory bandwidth for small-block cached reads is strong, but large-block uncached reads and writes drop to average levels. For LLM inference, the server achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small 135M models, but drops to a weak rating for large 70B model text generation.

Given its balanced vCPU-to-memory ratio and strong multi-threaded capabilities, the D48as_v6 is suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale web serving. Its qualitative cost efficiency is driven by its dedicated AMD EPYC hardware class, offering high resource density for multi-threaded tasks without the overhead of local storage or specialized GPU accelerators.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 x86_64 processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores, a 3.7 GHz clock speed, and 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core execution, alongside strong small-block memory bandwidth. However, large-block memory bandwidth and large-model LLM text generation are average to weak. This server is suited for database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose multi-threaded workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suited for database operations and software compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core benchmark results
Lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage disk attachments
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads
